task_objective: Land the lunar module safely between the flags, centered, slowly, upright, fuel-efficiently, and end with stable ground contact.

phase_structure.purpose: encourage measurable progress toward task completion
phase_structure.reward_intent: reduce horizontal and vertical distance to target pad while keeping velocities low

phase_structure.purpose: encourage safe/controlled behavior while progress is happening
phase_structure.reward_intent: maintain upright orientation and low angular motion
trigger large positive reward when both legs contact ground with low speed and upright attitude; penalize crash with body contacting ground without proper leg contact

component_blueprint.name: r_progress_guidance
component_blueprint.design_intent: Provide a continuous cost minimizing distance to pad center and altitude, plus velocity magnitudes, creating a gradient toward landing.
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: The reward is always non-positive; prolonged states near the ground yield only small negative cumulative cost, so the terminal success reward far outweighs delayed landing, incentivizing completion.

component_blueprint.name: r_stability
component_blueprint.design_intent: Penalize tilt angle and angular velocity to keep the lander upright and steady.
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: No state can permanently minimize this penalty without being upright near the pad, so it supports progress rather than replacing it.

component_blueprint.name: r_control_cost
component_blueprint.design_intent: Penalize engine thrusts to encourage fuel efficiency.
component_blueprint.anti_exploit_note: Side engine penalty uses absolute value to prevent any signed thrust direction from being rewarded.

anti_exploit_principles: All dense shaping rewards are non-positive so that net positive return comes only from the terminal success event, preventing indefinite reward farming.
Stability and alignment signals are designed as penalties; they support progress toward a good landing but do not provide positive credit that could be exploited without landing.
Action cost for signed actions uses absolute value to ensure penalty for effort magnitude, never a reward for thrust in one direction.
